What we do

Risk-focused engagements for measurable impact

Three connected services — from understanding risk before loss, through structured improvement, to expert guidance on the decisions that matter.

01 · Assess

Property Risk Assessment

Understanding risk before loss
  • On-site surveys & inspections
  • Risk identification & grading
  • Loss scenario analysis — PML / MFL
  • NFPA / FM Global / EN / VdS / BYKHY review
  • Photographic documentation
02 · Improve

Risk Improvement Programmes

From risk to resilience — through action
  • Fire protection systems review
  • Electrical safety assessment
  • Process & operational risk review
  • Prioritised improvement recommendations
  • Cost-effective mitigation planning
03 · Advise

Technical Risk Consulting

Expert guidance for complex decisions
  • Risk engineering advisory
  • Protection strategy development
  • Due diligence support
  • Underwriter & broker technical support
  • Risk review for acquisitions & new builds
How an engagement runs
01

Inspection

Site survey & data collection. We walk the facility and gather evidence directly.

02

Assessment

Risk evaluation & analysis against the applicable standards and loss scenarios.

03

Recommendation

Prioritised improvement plan — each item costed and referenced to a standard.

04

Implementation

Risk reduction & follow-up, with verification at the next survey cycle.

Risk Intelligence

Beyond reporting — risk translated into a defensible score

The RAS-SCORE™ combines five weighted factors into a single 0–100 index that maps to a clear A–E grade — and shows the improvement available once recommendations are closed.

Five weighted factors

Construction, Occupancy, Process, Protection and Exposure (C·O·P·Pr·E) — each scored on site, then weighted.

A single 0–100 index → A–E grade

The weighted factors resolve to one number and one clear grade an underwriter can read at a glance.

As-surveyed ↔ after-improvement

The score shows today's position and the grade achievable once the prioritised recommendations are closed.

RAS-SCORE™ · Five-factor index
0.0
/ 100 · weighted
—
C Construction—
O Occupancy—
P Process—
Pr Protection—
E Exposure—
ABCDE
Representative plastics plant · weights C15 O15 P20 Pr35 E15 · illustrative
Every risk we identify comes with a clear path forward.
— The RAS approach
Why RAS

The difference site experience makes

01

Site-first, always

Every assessment begins and ends on-site. We walk the facility, observe live operations, and gather evidence directly — not a checklist filled in from a desk.

02

Built for every stakeholder

Insurer, broker or operator — one site-based assessment carries the technical depth each needs to underwrite, advise or manage risk with confidence.

03

Outputs written to be acted on

We write for decision-makers, not filing cabinets. Every risk arrives with a realistic, prioritised, standard-referenced improvement — and a target grade once closed.

04

Multi-sector perspective

Assessed across 14+ sectors and hundreds of facilities, we bring comparative depth single-industry specialists can't. We know what good risk management looks like.

Meet the founder

Underwriting judgement and site engineering, in one pair of eyes

RAS was founded by a risk engineer whose work is the site assessment itself — walking the facility, grading exposure and writing a decision-ready report. Earlier fire-underwriting experience shapes how he reads a site, not what RAS sells: the focus is always the survey, never pricing or placement. That underwriting-informed eye, combined with multi-sector site work, shapes every engagement.

A report should answer the underwriter's actual question — accept, on what terms, and what to fix first.

Get in touch

Let's discuss your risks

Every facility carries a unique combination of operational, structural and systemic risk. Our approach starts with listening — then with seeing for ourselves. Tell us about the site and we'll propose a scope.
